claim: Agent quality requires both observability and evaluations: tracing for visibility, offline evaluations for regression testing, online evaluations for production monitoring
actionable: Implement three-layer quality assurance for agents: tracing in development, offline evals before deployment, and online monitors in production
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Samraj Moorjani: Accelerate High quality Agent Development with MLflow

claim: Context rot (performance degradation with large context) remains critical even with 1M token limits - larger windows harm LLM recall accuracy
actionable: Even with large context windows, keep agent context lean by removing irrelevant information to maintain LLM recall accuracy
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Samraj Moorjani: Accelerate High quality Agent Development with MLflow

claim: Agent as a judge pattern allows expressing complex evaluation criteria in natural language while the judge traverses traces to extract needed context
actionable: Use agent-as-judge to evaluate complex agent behaviors by expressing criteria in English rather than code
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Samraj Moorjani: Accelerate High quality Agent Development with MLflow

claim: MLflow tracing supports 25+ agentic frameworks and model providers with one-line implementation, covering 90%+ of the ecosystem
actionable: Use MLflow for agent observability to get unified tracing across your entire agent stack with minimal code changes
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Samraj Moorjani: Accelerate High quality Agent Development with MLflow

claim: Agentic insights automatically analyzes production traces to identify issues and root causes, eliminating manual log analysis at scale
actionable: Use agentic insights to automatically surface top issues in production traces rather than manually parsing logs
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Samraj Moorjani: Accelerate High quality Agent Development with MLflow

claim: Judges trained with human feedback initially achieve 50% agreement but can reach 100% alignment through feedback-driven iteration
actionable: Iteratively align evaluation judges with domain expert feedback to improve evaluation reliability from initial poor agreement to perfect alignment
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Samraj Moorjani: Accelerate High quality Agent Development with MLflow

claim: Prompt optimization (like JEPA) can migrate agents to cheaper models while preserving quality by using feedback signals from evaluations
actionable: Use automatic prompt optimization to migrate agents to cheaper models without quality regression
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Samraj Moorjani: Accelerate High quality Agent Development with MLflow

claim: Out-of-the-box evaluation judges for hallucination detection (groundedness/faithfulness) exist and should be applied in both development and production
actionable: Apply groundedness and faithfulness judges early in development to catch hallucinations before production deployment
source_title: AI Dev 25 x NYC | Samraj Moorjani: Accelerate High quality Agent Development with MLflow
